failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await               label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n fence_context = {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                    )\n                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n _component_library.close()\n197\t    if _store:\n198\t        await _store.close()\n199\t    logger.info(\"aide-de-camp shutdown complete\")\n200\t\n201\t\n202\tapp = FastAPI(title=\"ADC Generate utterance ID if not provided\n437\t    if not utterance_id:\n438\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n439\t\n440\t    # Create utterance record\n441\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n442\t\n443\t    # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n465\t            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n466\t            intent_results.append(result)\n467\t\n468\t        return {\n469\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n470\t            \"intents\": intent_results,\n471\t        }\n472\t\n473\t    except Exception as e:\n474\t        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n475\t        return JSONResponse(\n476\t            status_code=500,\n477\t            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n478\t        )\n479\t\n480\t\n481\t@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\n482\tasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n483\t    \"\"\"\n484\t    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n485\t\n486\t    Phase 0 core query loop:\n487\t    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n488\t    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n489\t    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n490\t    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n491\t\n492\t    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n493\t    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n494\t    \"\"\"\n495\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n496\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n497\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n498\t    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n499\t\n500\t    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n501\t\n502\t    store = await get_store()\n503\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n504\t\n505\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n506\t    if not utterance_id:\n507\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n508\t\n509\t    # Create utterance record\n510\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n511\t\n512\t    # Route the utterance\n513\t    try:\n514\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n515\t            utterance=utterance,\n516\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n517\t            session_id=session_id,\n518\t        )\n519\t\n520\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n521\t        intent_tasks = []\n522\t        intent_ids = []\n523\t\n524\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n525\t            # Create intent record in store\n526\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n527\t            await store.create_intent(\n528\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n529\t                session_id=session_id,\n530\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n531\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n532\t                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n533\t            )\n534\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n535\t\n536\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n537\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n538\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n539\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n540\t            )\n541\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n542\t\n543\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n544\t        async def stream_results():\n545\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n546\t            results = []\n547\t\n548\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n549\t                try:\n550\t                    result = await task\n551\t                    results.append(result)\n552\t\n553\t                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n554\t                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n555\t                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n556\t                        # Include rendered card HTML in SSE so canvas injects it directly\n557\t                        # (Component card when matched, fallback when card_fallback=True)\n558\t                        sse_data = {\n559\t                            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n560\t                            \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n561\t                            \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n562\t                            \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n563\t                        }\n564\t                        # Add component_id when available (for canvas tracking)\n565\t                        if result.get(\"component_id\") is not None:\n566\t                            sse_data[\"component_id\"] = result[\"component_id\"]\n567\t                        # Add card_fallback flag when available (signals client to use fallback)\n568\t                        if result.get(\"card_fallback\") is not None:\n569\t                            sse_data[\"card_fallback\"] = result[\"card_fallback\"]\n570\t\n571\t                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n572\t                            SSEEvent(\n573\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n574\t                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n575\t                                data=sse_data,\n576\t                                rendered_html=result.get(\"rendered_html\"),\n577\t                            )\n578\t                        )\n579\t                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n580\t                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n581\t                        try:\n582\t                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n583\t                                intent_id,\n584\t                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n585\t                            )\n586\t                        except Exception as te:\n587\t                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n588\t\n589\t                except Exception as e:\n590\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n591\t                    results.append({\n592\t                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n593\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n594\t                        \"error\": str(e),\n595\t                    })\n596\t\n597\t            return results\n598\t\n599\t        # Start parallel processing in background\n600\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n601\t\n602\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n603\t        return {\n604\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n605\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n606\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n607\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n608\t            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n609\t            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n610\t        }\n611\t\n612\t    except Exception as e:\n613\t        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n614\t        return JSONResponse(\n615\t            status_code=500,\n616\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n617\t        )\n618\t\n619\t\n620\t@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\n621\tasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n622\t    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n623\t\n624\t    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n625\t    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n626\t    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n627\t    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n628\t    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n629\t    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n630\t    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n631\t    stay NULL.\n632\t\n633\t    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n634\t    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n635\t    \"\"\"\n636\t    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n637\t    if not intent_id:\n638\t        return JSONResponse(\n639\t            status_code=400,\n640\t            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n641\t        )\n642\t\n643\t    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n644\t    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n645\t        value = request.get(name)\n646\t        if value is not None:\n647\t            fields[name] = int(value)\n648\t\n649\t    store = await get_store()\n650\t    try:\n651\t        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n652\t        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n653\t        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n654\t        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n655\t    except Exception as e:\n656\t        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n657\t        return JSONResponse(\n658\t            status_code=500,\n659\t            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n660\t        )\n661\t\n662\t    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n663\t\n664\t\n665\t@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\n666\tasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n667\t    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per Register change_voice tool\n        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"change_voice\",\n            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            results = []\n\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n\n                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n                        # Include rendered card HTML in SSE so canvas injects it directly\n                        # (Component card when matched, fallback when card_fallback=True)\n                        sse_data = {\n                            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                            \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n                            \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n                            \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n                        }\n                        # Add component_id when available (for canvas tracking)\n                        if result.get(\"component_id\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"component_id\"] = result[\"component_id\"]\n                        # Add card_fallback flag when available (signals client to use fallback)\n                        if result.get(\"card_fallback\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"card_fallback\"] = result[\"card_fallback\"]\n\n                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n                            SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                                data=sse_data,\n                                rendered_html=result.get(\"rendered_html\"),\n                            )\n                        )\n                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n                        try:\n                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n                                intent_id,\n                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n                            )\n                        except Exception as te:\n                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n\n            return results\n\n        # Start parallel processing in background\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\nasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n\n    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n    stay NULL.\n\n    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n    \"\"\"\n    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n    if not intent_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n        )\n\n    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n        value = request.get(name)\n        if value is not None:\n            fields[name] = int(value)\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    try:\n        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n        )\n\n    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\nasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per dispatch stage (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n\n    Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n    that has at least one captured sample. The latency-baseline bead consumes\n    the un-windowed store helper directly; this endpoint exposes the same\n    numbers over HTTP for the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist and on-demand\n    inspection. Optional ``since`` (unix timestamp) windows to recent\n    dispatches only.\n    \"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n    return await store.get_latency_percentiles(since=since)\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async handling.\n\n    For task-profile intents that require tracking and async execution:\n    - Formulates bead body via LLM\n    - Creates bead via bf CLI\n    - Returns pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    intent_type = request.get(\"intent_type\", \"task-profile\")\n    project_slug = request.get(\"project_slug\")\n    topic_id = request.get(\"topic_id\")\n    metadata = request.get(\"metadata\", {})\n    context = request.get(\"context\", {})\n\n    logger.info(f\"Escalating intent for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Create escalate request\n    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n        intent_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),\n        session_id=session_id,\n        utterance=utterance,\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        project_slug=project_slug,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n        context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = intent_id\n\n    try:\n        # Escalate to bead\n        result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {escalate_request.intent_id} to bead {result.bead_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": result.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n            \"status\": result.status,\n            \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/environment\")\nasync def get_environment():\n    \"\"\"Return the discovered environment: all git repos and bead workspaces across all hosts.\"\"\"\n    from .environment.discovery import get_registry\n    import time\n    registry = get_registry()\n    if not registry:\n        return {\"error\": \"Registry not yet initialized\"}\n    entries = [\n        {\n            \"name\": e.name,\n            \"slug\": e.slug,\n            \"path\": str(e.path),\n            \"host\": e.host,\n            \"display_path\": e.display_path,\n            \"has_beads\": e.has_beads,\n            \"remote_url\": e.remote_url,\n            \"remote_name\": e.remote_name,\n        }\n        for e in sorted(registry.all_entries(), key=lambda x: (x.host or \"\", x.slug))\n    ]\n    summary = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"total_repos\": summary[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": summary[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": summary[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": summary[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": summary[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"last_scan_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n        \"repos\": entries,\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/registry\")\nasync def get_registry_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Return → Built-in cards, and Cold\n    start & demo seed). return {\"projects\": projects}\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/environment/refresh\")\nasync def trigger_environment_refresh():\n    \"\"\"Trigger an immediate environment rescan to discover new repos.\"\"\"\n    import time\n    registry = await refresh_registry()\n    s = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"status\": \"refreshed\",\n        \"total_repos\": s[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": s[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": s[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": s[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": s[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"scanned_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# API v1 Endpoints\n# =============================================================================\n\n# Pydantic models for API requests/responses\nclass SurfaceRegisterRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n\n\nclass HeartbeatRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n\n\nclass FeedbackRequestModel(BaseModel):\n    feedback: str\n    feedback_type: str = \"self_modification\"\n    context: Optional[dict] = None\n    session_id: Optional[str] = None\n    require_approval: bool = True\n\n\nclass ApprovalRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n\n\nclass BeadApproveRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n    session_id: str\n\n\nclass BeadRejectRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    reason: Optional[str] = None\n\n\nclass RollbackRequest(BaseModel):\n    artifact_name: str\n    artifact_type: str\n\n\nclass ComponentCreateRequest(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    description: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str = \"Initial version\"\n\n\nclass ComponentUpdateRequest(BaseModel):\n    component_id: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str\n\n\nclass ComponentIterateRequest(BaseModel):\n    component_id: str\n    feedback: str\n    result_data: Optional[dict] = None\n\n\nclass UsagePatternRecord(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"Request body for recording component usage patterns.\"\"\"\n    component_id: str\n    result_type: str\n    match_score: float\n    layout_bucket: str = \"normal\"\n\n\n# Canvas and Surface endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\")\nasync def api_v1_register_surface(request: SurfaceRegisterRequest):\n    \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n    else:\n        session_id = request.session_id\n\n    # Register surface\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(\n        session_id,\n        request.surface_type\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"session_id\": session_id\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics\")\nasync def api_v1_get_session_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"Get active topic cards for a session (canvas session_id: str,\n    surface_id: Optional[str] = None,\n    surface_type: str = \"canvas\"\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await           yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve and apply a pending change.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not response.message,\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/feedback/pending\")\nasync def api_v1_list_pending():\n    \"\"\"List all pending feedback approvals.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    return {\n        \"pending\": version.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # def api_v1_approve_bead(request: BeadApproveRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve a pending bead and create it via bf CLI.\n\n    Takes an approval_id from a pending_bead_approvals row, creates the bead,\n    and returns the pending card for the surface.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .escalate.handler import get_escalate_handler, EscalateRequest\n    import json\n\n    store = session_store_get_store()\n\n    # Get the pending approval\n    approval = await store.get_pending_approval(request.approval_id)\n    if not approval:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=404,\n            content={\"error\": \"Approval not found or expired\"}\n        )\n\n    if approval[\"status\"] != \"pending\":\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Approval already {approval['status']}\"}\n        )\n\n    if approval[\"session_id\"] != request.session_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=403,\n            content={\"error\": \"Session mismatch\"}\n        )\n\n    logger.info(f\"Approving bead {request.approval_id} for intent {approval['intent_id']}\")\n\n    try:\n        # Create the bead using the escalate handler\n        escalate_handler = get_escalate_handler(store)\n\n        # Build an escalate request from the approval data\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=approval[\"intent_id\"],\n            session_id=approval[\"session_id\"],\n            utterance=approval[\"utterance\"],\n            intent_type=\"action\",  # Default to action for approved beads\n            project_slug=approval[\"project_slug\"],\n            topic_id=approval[\"topic_id\"],\n        )\n\n        # Create the bead with the stored body\n        bead_id = await escalate_handler._create_bead_with_type(\n            request=escalate_request,\n            bead_body=approval[\"bead_body\"],\n            bead_type=approval[\"bead_type\"],\n        )\n\n        # Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n        await escalate_handler._create_bead_watch(\n            bead_ref=bead_id,\n            project_slug=approval[\"project_slug\"],\n            intent_type=\"action\",\n        )\n\n        # Update approval status\n        await store.update_approval_status(request.approval_id, \"approved\")\n\n        # Update intent status\n        await store.update_intent_status(\n            intent_id=approval[\"intent_id\"],\n            status=\"dispatched\",\n        )\n\n        # Build pending card\n        pending_card = escalate_handler.build_pending_card(\n            request=escalate_request,\n            bead_id=bead_id,\n            bead_type=approval[\"bead_type\"],\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast result_created event to canvas\n        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n            SSEEvent(\n                event_type=EventType.RESULT_CREATED,\n                data={\n                    \"intent_id\": approval[\"intent_id\"],\n                    \"session_id\": approval[\"session_id\"],\n                    \"result_id\": f\"result-{bead_id}\",  # Use bead_id as result_id\n                    \"topic_id\": approval[\"topic_id\"],\n                    \"summary\": pending_card[\"summary\"],\n                    \"urgency\": pending_card[\"urgency\"],\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                },\n                target_session_id=approval[\"session_id\"],\n            )\n        )\n\n        logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} from approval {request.approval_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"approved\",\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"pending_card\": pending_card,\n            \"message\": f\"Bead {bead_id} created successfully\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to approve bead {request.approval_id}: {e}\")\n        # Update approval status to rejected on error\n        await store.update_approval_status(request.approval_id, \"rejected\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to create bead: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/beads/reject\")\nasync def api_v1_reject_bead(request: BeadRejectRequest):\n    \"\"\"Reject a pending bead approval.\n\n    Marks the approval as rejected and updates the intent status.\n    \"\"\"\n    store = session_store_get_store()\n\n    # Get the pending approval\n    approval = await store.get_pending_approval(request.approval_id)\n =============================================================================\n# Usage patterns routes (must come before /{component_id} routes to avoid conflicts)\n# =============================================================================\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/patterns\")\nasync def api_v1_record_pattern(request: UsagePatternRecord):\n    \"\"\"Record or update a component usage pattern.\n\n    Simple endpoint for recording component usage patterns.\n    Upserts pattern (update if exists, insert if new) and sets updated_at to current timestamp.\n\n    Request body:\n        - result_type: str - The type of result\n        - component_id: str - The component that was used\n        - layout_bucket: str - The layout bucket used (default 'normal')\n        - match_score: float - How well the component matched (0.0-1.0)\n\n    Returns:\n        {\"status\": \"ok\", \"pattern\": {...}} on success\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        # Validate inputs\n        if not request.component_id or not request.result_type:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": \"component_id and result_type are required\"}\n            )\n\n        if not 0.0 <= request.match_score <= 1.0:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": \"match_score must be between 0.0 and 1.0\"}\n            )\n\n        if request.layout_bucket not in _component_library.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": f\"layout_bucket must be one of {', '.join(_component_library.LAYOUT_BUCKETS)}\"}\n            )\n\n        # Record the pattern (upsert: update if exists, insert if new)\n        _component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=request.component_id,\n            result_type=request.result_type,\n            match_score=request.match_score,\n            layout_bucket=request.layout_bucket,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Recorded usage pattern: component={request.component_id}, \"\n            f\"result_type={request.result_type}, layout_bucket={request.layout_bucket}, \"\n            f\"match_score={request.match_score}\"\n        )\n\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"ok\",\n            \"pattern\": {\n                \"component_id\": request.component_id,\n                \"result_type\": request.result_type,\n                \"layout_bucket\": request.layout_bucket,\n                \"match_score\": request.match_score,\n            }\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to record usage pattern: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record usage pattern: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/patterns\")\nasync def api_v1_get_patterns(result_type: str = Query(..., description=\"Result type to filter patterns (required)\")):\n    \"\"\"Get component usage patterns by result_type.\n\n    Query params:\n        result_type: Filter by result type (required)\n\n    Returns:\n        {\"patterns\": [...]} ordered by match_score DESC\n        404 if no patterns found for the result_type\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        import sqlite3\n\n        conn = _component_library._get_conn()\n\n        # Query patterns for the specified result_type, ordered by match_score DESC\n        query = \"\"\"\n            SELECT result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count, updated_at\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n        \"\"\"\n\n        rows = conn.execute(query, (result_type,)).fetchall()\n\n        # Return 404 if no patterns found\n        if not rows:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=404,\n                content={\"error\": f\"No patterns found for result_type: {result_type}\"}\n            )\n\n        patterns = [\n            {\n                \"result_type\": row[0],\n                \"component_id\": row[1],\n                \"layout_bucket\": row[2],\n                \"match_score\": row[3],\n                \"sample_count\": row[4],\n                \"updated_at\": row[5],\n            }\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n        return {\n            \"patterns\": patterns,\n            \"count\": len(patterns),\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to get usage patterns: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to get usage patterns: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/components/usage-patterns\")\nasync def api_v1_record_usage_pattern(request: UsagePatternRecord):\n    \"\"\"Record or update a component This endpoint updates the\n    component_usage_patterns table with the latest match score and usage data.\n\n    Returns:\n        {\"status\": \"ok\", \"pattern\": {...}} on success\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        # Validate inputs\n        if not request.component_id or not request.result_type:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": \"component_id and result_type are required\"}\n            )\n\n        if not 0.0 <= request.match_score <= 1.0:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": \"match_score must be between 0.0 and 1.0\"}\n            )\n\n        if request.layout_bucket not in _component_library.LAYOUT_BUCKETS:\n            return JSONResponse(\n                status_code=400,\n                content={\"error\": f\"layout_bucket must be one of {', '.join(_component_library.LAYOUT_BUCKETS)}\"}\n            )\n\n        # Record the pattern\n        _component_library.record_usage_pattern(\n            component_id=request.component_id,\n            result_type=request.result_type,\n            match_score=request.match_score,\n api_v1_list_usage_patterns(\n    result_type: Optional[str] = Query(None, description=\"Filter by result type\"),\n    component_id: Optional[str] = Query(None, description=\"Filter by component ID\"),\n    limit: int = Query(100, description=\"Maximum number of patterns to return\"),\n):\n    \"\"\"List component usage patterns, optionally filtered.\n\n    Query params:\n        result_type: Filter by result type (optional)\n        component_id: Filter by component ID (optional)\n        limit: Maximum number of patterns to return (default 100)\n\n    Returns:\n        {\"patterns\": [...], \"count\": <total>}\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    try:\n        import sqlite3\n\n        conn = _component_library._get_conn()\n\n        # Build query with filters\n        where_clauses = []\n        params = []\n\n        if result_type:\n            where_clauses.append(\"result_type = ?\")\n            params.append(result_type)\n\n        if component_id:\n            where_clauses.append(\"component_id = ?\")\n            params.append(component_id)\n\n        where_sql = \" AND \".join(where_clauses) if where_clauses else \"1=1\"\n\n        query = f\"\"\"\n            SELECT result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count, updated_at\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE {where_sql}\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT ?\n        \"\"\"\n\n        params.append(limit)\n\n        rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()\n\n        patterns = [\n            {\n                \"result_type\": row[0],\n                \"component_id\": row[1],\n                \"layout_bucket\": row[2],\n                \"match_score\": row[3],\n                \"sample_count\": row[4],\n                \"updated_at\": row[5],\n            }\n            for row in rows\n        ]\n\n        # Get total count\n        count_query = f\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM component_usage_patterns WHERE {where_sql}\"\n        count_params = params[:-1]  # Exclude limit\n        total = conn.execute(count_query, count_params).fetchone()[0]\n\n        return {\n            \"patterns\": patterns,\n            \"count\": total,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to list usage patterns: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to list usage patterns: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t/**\n66\t * Build a topic card from topic data.\n67\t *\n68\t * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n69\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n70\t */\n71\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n72\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n73\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n74\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n75\t    const cardId = cardData.card_id;  // Unique (topic, result_type) identifier\n76\t\n77\t    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n78\t    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n79\t    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n80\t    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n81\t    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n82\t    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n83\t        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n84\t        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n85\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n86\t        fallbackCard.dataset.cardId = cardId;\n87\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n88\t        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n89\t        return fallbackCard;\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    // When the result has rendered_html (from hot-path component match), inject it directly.\n93\t    // The HTML is already escaped server-side (hot-path renderer's escaping contract),\n94\t    // so we inject it as-is. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n * escaping contract.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n */\n\n// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n    var document = global.document;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\nvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Existing topic-card render helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a topic card from topic data.\n *\n * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n */\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n    const cardId = cardData.card_id;  // Unique (topic, result_type) identifier\n\n    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n        fallbackCard.dataset.cardId = cardId;\n        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n        return fallbackCard;\n    }\n\n    // When the result has rendered_html (from hot-path component match), inject it directly.\n    // The HTML is already escaped server-side (hot-path renderer's escaping contract),\n    // so we inject it as-is.           (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n185\t        \"\"\"\n186\t        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n187\t\n188\t        # Extract context for rule }\n196\t\n197\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n198\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n199\t        action =         actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n208\t\n209\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n210\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and Supports simple conditions like:\n243\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n244\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n245\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n246\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n247\t\n248\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n249\t\n250\t        Args:\n251\t            condition: The condition string\n252\t            context: Dict of available variables\n253\t\n254\t        Returns:\n255\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n256\t        \"\"\"\n257\t        try:\n258\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n259\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n260\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n261\t\n262\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n263\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n264\t            safe_locals = {\n265\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n266\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n267\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n268\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n269\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n270\t            }\n271\t\n272\t            # Evaluate condition\n273\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n274\t            return bool(result)\n275\t        except Exception as e:\n276\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n277\t            return False\n278\t\n279\t    def request: EscalateRequest,\n439\t    ) -> str:\n440\t        \"\"\"\n441\t        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n442\t\n443\t        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n444\t        \"\"\"\n445\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n446\t        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n447\t\n448\t        # Build context for the LLM\n449\t  model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n483\t                max_tokens=4096,\n484\t                temperature=0.7,\n485\t            )\n486\t\n487\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n488\t            return bead_body\n489\t\n490\t        except Exception as e:\n491\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n492\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n493\t\n494\t    async def create_bead(\n495\t        self,\n496\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n497\t        bead_body: str,\n498\t    ) -> str:\n499\t        \"\"\"\n500\t        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n501\t\n502\t        Returns the bead ID.\n503\t        \"\"\"\n504\t        # Generate            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n510\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n511\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n512\t            \"origin_surface_id\": metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n518\t\n519\t        if request.topic_id:\n520\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n521\t\n522\t        # Build bf create \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n532\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n533\t            ]\n534\t\n535\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n536\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n537\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n538\t                if value is not None:\n539\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n540\t\n541\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n542\t                *cmd,\n543\t _create_bead_watch(\n640\t        self,\n641\t        bead_ref: str,\n642\t        project_slug: str | None,\n643\t        intent_type: str,\n644\t    ) -> None:\n645\t        \"\"\"Create bead_watch
