str:\n679\t        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n680\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n681\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n682\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n683\t            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n684\t        else:\n685\t            title = utterance\n686\t\n687\t        # Add project prefix if available\n688\t        if request.project_slug:\n689\t            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n690\t\n691\t        return title\n692\t\n693\t    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n694\t        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n695\t\n696\t        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n697\t        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n698\t        \"\"\"\n699\t        import re\n700\t\n701\t        # Strip whitespace first\n702\t        output = output.strip()\n703\t\n704\t        if not output:\n705\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n706\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n707\t\n708\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n709\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n710\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n711\t        patterns = [\n712\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n713\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n714\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n715\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n716\t        ]\n717\t\n718\t        for pattern in patterns:\n719\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n720\t            if matches:\n721\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n722\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n723\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n724\t                return bead_id\n725\t\n726\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n727\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n728\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n729\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n730\t            return output\n731\t\n732\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n733\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n734\t        return output\n735\t\n736\t    async def _validate_and_prepare_approval(\n737\t        self,\n738\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n739\t        bead_body: str,\n740\t        bead_type: str,\n741\t    ) -> tuple[ValidationResult, str | None]:\n742\t        \"\"\"\n743\t        Validate bead body and prepare for approval if needed.\n744\t\n745\t        Args:\n746\t            request: The escalate request\n747\t            bead_body: The formulated bead body\n748\t            bead_type: The bead type\n749\t\n750\t        Returns:\n751\t            (validation_result, reformulated_bead_body_or_None)\n752\t\n753\t        Raises:\n754\t            BeadApprovalRequired: When bead requires approval (carries approval card)\n755\t            ValidationRetryExhaustedError: When validation fails after re-formulation\n756\t        \"\"\"\n757\t        validator = get_validator()\n758\t\n759\t        # Initial validation\n760\t        result = validator.validate_bead_body(bead_body, bead_type)\n761\t\n762\t        if result.is_valid and not result.requires_approval:\n763\t            logger.info(f\"Validation passed for intent {request.intent_id} (no approval required)\")\n764\t            return result, None\n765\t\n766\t        if result.requires_approval:\n767\t            logger.info(f\"Validation passed but approval required for intent {request.intent_id}: {result.approval_requirement.reason}\")\n768\t            # Build approval card\n769\t            approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n770\t                request=request,\n771\t                bead_body=bead_body,\n772\t                bead_type=bead_type,\n773\t                validation_result=result,\n774\t            )\n775\t            raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, bead_body, bead_type)\n776\t\n777\t        # Validation failed - check re-formulation count before attempting\n778\t        store = await self._get_store()\n779\t        reformulation_count = await store.get_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n780\t        MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS = 3  # Maximum 3 attempts per session\n781\t\n782\t        if reformulation_count >= MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS:\n783\t            logger.error(\n784\t                f\"Session {request.session_id} has exceeded re-formulation attempt limit \"\n785\t                f\"({reformulation_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\"\n786\t            )\n787\t            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n788\t                result.violations,\n789\t                f\"Re-formulation attempt limit exceeded ({MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS} attempts per session). \"\n790\t                \"Please clarify your request or provide more specific details.\",\n791\t            )\n792\t\n793\t        # Increment re-formulation count\n794\t        new_count = await store.increment_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n795\t        logger.info(f\"Attempt re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id} (session attempt {new_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\")\n796\t        logger.warning(f\"Validation failed for intent {request.intent_id}, attempting re-formulation\")\n797\t        logger.info(f\"Reformulation hint: {result.reformulation_hint}\")\n798\t\n799\t        try:\n800\t            # Re-formulate with failure reason\n801\t            reformulated_body = await self._reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n802\t                request=request,\n803\t                original_body=bead_body,\n804\t                validation_result=result,\n805\t            )\n806\t\n807\t            # Validate the reformulated body\n808\t            new_result = validator.validate_bead_body(reformulated_body, bead_type)\n809\t\n810\t            if not new_result.is_valid:\n811\t                # Re-formulation still failed\n812\t                logger.error(f\"Re-formulation still failed for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n813\t                raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n814\t                    new_result.violations,\n815\t                    f\"Validation failed after re-formulation: {len(new_result.violations)} violations remain\",\n816\t                )\n817\t\n818\t            if new_result.requires_approval:\n819\t                logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body requires approval\")\n820\t                approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n821\t                    request=request,\n822\t                    bead_body=reformulated_body,\n823\t                    bead_type=bead_type,\n824\t                    validation_result=new_result,\n825\t                )\n826\t                raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, reformulated_body, bead_type)\n827\t\n828\t            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body passed validation\")\n829\t            return new_result, reformulated_body\n830\t\n831\t        except Exception as e:\n832\t            logger.error(f\"Re-formulation failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n833\t            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n834\t                result.violations,\n835\t                f\"Re-formulation failed: {str(e)}\",\n836\t            )\n837\t\n838\t    async def _reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n839\t        self,\n840\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n841\t        original_body: str,\n842\t        validation_result: ValidationResult,\n843\t    ) -> str:\n844\t        \"\"\"\n845\t        Re-formulate bead body with validation failure reason.\n846\t\n847\t        Args:\n848\t            request: The escalate request\n849\t            original_body: The original bead body\n850\t            validation_result: The validation result with violations\n851\t\n852\t        Returns:\n853\t            Reformulated bead body\n854\t        \"\"\"\n855\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n856\t        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n857\t\n858\t        # Build failure message\n859\t        failure_message = \"The previous bead body failed validation:\\n\\n\"\n860\t        for violation in validation_result.violations:\n861\t            failure_message += f\"- {violation.message}\\n\"\n862\t        if validation_result.reformulation_hint:\n863\t            failure_message += f\"\\nHint: {validation_result.reformulation_hint}\"\n864\t\n865\t        # Build user message\n866\t        user_message = f\"\"\"Original intent: Structures the work for Claude Code execution\n\nOutput ONLY the bead body as markdown. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_escalate_prompt() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Load the escalate prompt from the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH, \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            if content.strip():\n                return content\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt file is empty: {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n                return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt not found at {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n        return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateRequest:\n    \"\"\"Request to escalate an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    intent_type: str\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    topic_id: str | None = None\n    context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"session_id\": self.session_id,\n            \"utterance\": self.utterance,\n            \"intent_type\": self.intent_type,\n            \"project_slug\": self.project_slug,\n            \"topic_id\": self.topic_id,\n            \"context\": self.context,\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of escalating an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    bead_id: str\n    intent_id: str\n    pending_card: dict\n    status: str = \"pending\"  # pending, created, failed\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"bead_id\": self.bead_id,\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"pending_card\": self.pending_card,\n            \"status\": self.status,\n        }\n\n\nclass EscalateError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for escalate errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass BeadCreationError(EscalateError):\n    \"\"\"Bead creation failed.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass BeadApprovalRequired(EscalateError):\n    \"\"\"Bead requires user approval before creation.\n\n    Raised when validation passes but requires user approval.\n    The approval card details are carried in the exception.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, approval_card: dict, bead_body: str, bead_type: str):\n        self.approval_card = approval_card\n        self.bead_body = bead_body\n        self.bead_type = bead_type\n        super().__init__(\"Bead requires user approval\")\n\n\nclass EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate handler for task-profile intents.\n\n    Handles the full escalate flow:\n    1. Check auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n    2. Either execute directly (auto-approved) OR create bead (manual approval)\n    3. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n        \"\"\"\n        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n\n        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n        context = {\n            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n        }\n\n        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n        action = context.get(\"action\")\n        if action in read_only_actions:\n            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n\n        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n        for rule in safe_mutations:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n\n        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n        for rule in manual_rules:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n            if action not in actions:\n                continue\n\n            # Evaluate the condition\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n\n        # Check approval workflow rules\n        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n\n        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n\n    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n\n        Supports simple conditions like:\n        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n\n        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n\n        Args:\n            condition: The condition string\n            context: Dict of available variables\n\n        Returns:\n            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n\n            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n            safe_locals = {\n                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n            }\n\n            # Evaluate condition\n            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n            return bool(result)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n        intent_type_mapping = {\n            \"action\": \"action\",\n            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n        }\n\n        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n\n        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n\n    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n\n        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n            else:\n                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n                return {\n                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n                    \"data\": {\n                        \"action\": action,\n                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n                    },\n                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n                }\n\n        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n\n    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n\n        # Parse utterance to extract pod name and namespace\n        params = executor.parse_delete_pod_utterance(\n            utterance=request.utterance,\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        # Execute the delete command\n        result = return result\n\n    async def _execute_kubectl_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute other kubectl commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement other kubectl commands\n        action = LLM.\n\n        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n        try:\n            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        try:\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", bead_type,\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_watch(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n\n        Plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA.\n        SLA defaults by intent_type, with per-project sla_hours override.\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n            project_slug: Project slug for SLA override lookup\n            intent_type: Intent type for default SLA lookup\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..registry import get_project\n\n        sla_hours = None\n\n        # Check for per-project SLA override\n        if project_slug:\n            project = get_project(project_slug)\n            if project:\n                sla_hours = project.get(\"sla_hours\")\n                if sla_hours is not None:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}: \"\n                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n                    )\n\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        await store.create_bead_watch(\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n\n        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Strip whitespace first\n        output = output.strip()\n\n        if not output:\n            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n        patterns = [\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n        ]\n\n        for pattern in patterns:\n            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n            if matches:\n                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n                return bead_id\n\n        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n            return output\n\n        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n        return output\n\n    async def _validate_and_prepare_approval(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> tuple[ValidationResult, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate bead body and prepare for approval if needed.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            bead_body: The formulated bead body\n            bead_type: The bead type\n\n        Returns:\n            (validation_result, reformulated_bead_body_or_None)\n\n        Raises:\n            BeadApprovalRequired: When bead requires approval (carries approval card)\n            ValidationRetryExhaustedError: When validation fails after re-formulation\n        \"\"\"\n        validator = get_validator()\n\n        # Initial validation\n        result = validator.validate_bead_body(bead_body, bead_type)\n\n        if result.is_valid and not result.requires_approval:\n            logger.info(f\"Validation passed for intent {request.intent_id} (no approval required)\")\n            return result, None\n\n        if result.requires_approval:\n            logger.info(f\"Validation passed but approval required for intent {request.intent_id}: {result.approval_requirement.reason}\")\n            # Build approval card\n            approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n                request=request,\n                bead_body=bead_body,\n                bead_type=bead_type,\n                validation_result=result,\n            )\n            raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, bead_body, bead_type)\n\n        # Validation failed - check re-formulation count before attempting\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        reformulation_count = await store.get_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n        MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS = 3  # Maximum 3 attempts per session\n\n        if reformulation_count >= MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"Session {request.session_id} has exceeded re-formulation attempt limit \"\n                f\"({reformulation_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\"\n            )\n            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n                result.violations,\n                f\"Re-formulation attempt limit exceeded ({MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS} attempts per session). \"\n                \"Please clarify your request or provide more specific details.\",\n            )\n\n        # Increment re-formulation count\n        new_count = await store.increment_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Attempt re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id} (session attempt {new_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\")\n        logger.warning(f\"Validation failed for intent {request.intent_id}, attempting re-formulation\")\n        logger.info(f\"Reformulation hint: {result.reformulation_hint}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Re-formulate with failure reason\n            reformulated_body = await self._reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n                request=request,\n                original_body=bead_body,\n                validation_result=result,\n            )\n\n            # Validate the reformulated body\n            new_result = validator.validate_bead_body(reformulated_body, bead_type)\n\n            if not new_result.is_valid:\n                # Re-formulation still failed\n                logger.error(f\"Re-formulation still failed for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n                raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n                    new_result.violations,\n                    f\"Validation failed after re-formulation: {len(new_result.violations)} violations remain\",\n                )\n\n            if new_result.requires_approval:\n                logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body requires approval\")\n                approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n                    request=request,\n                    bead_body=reformulated_body,\n                    bead_type=bead_type,\n                    validation_result=new_result,\n                )\n                raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, reformulated_body, bead_type)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body passed validation\")\n            return new_result, reformulated_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Re-formulation failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n                result.violations,\n                f\"Re-formulation failed: {str(e)}\",\n            )\n\n    async def _reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        original_body: str,\n        validation_result: ValidationResult,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Re-formulate bead body with validation failure reason.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            original_body: The original bead body\n            validation_result: The validation result with violations\n\n        Returns:\n            Reformulated bead body\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build failure message\n        failure_message = \"The previous bead body failed validation:\\n\\n\"\n        for violation in validation_result.violations:\n            failure_message += f\"- {violation.message}\\n\"\n        if validation_result.reformulation_hint:\n            failure_message += f\"\\nHint: {validation_result.reformulation_hint}\"\n\n        # Build user message\n        user_message = f\"\"\"Original intent: {request.utterance}\n\n{failure_message}\n\nPlease re-formulate the bead body to address these validation errors.\nOutput ONLY the revised bead body as markdown.\n\"\"\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Requesting re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            reformulated = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(reformulated)} chars\")\n            return reformulated\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to re-formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    def _build_approval_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n        validation_result: ValidationResult,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build an approval card for the surface.\n\n        The approval card shows the bead body and asks for user approval.\n        \"\"\"\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"approval\",\n            \"id\": f\"approval-{request.intent_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"title\": title,\n            \"summary\": f\"Review before creating: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"awaiting_approval\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"bead_body\": bead_body,\n            \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            \"validation_result\": validation_result.to_dict(),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n        }\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            },\n        }\n\n    def build_clarification_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        original_bead_body: str,\n        violations: list,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a clarification card for the surface.\n\n        The clarification card shows the user that the intent needs\n        clarification due to validation failures after re-formulation.\n        \"\"\"\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Format violations for display\n        violation_messages = []\n        for v in violations:\n            violation_messages.append(f\"- {v.message}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"id\": f\"clarification-{request.intent_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"title\": title,\n            \"summary\": f\"Needs clarification: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"needs_clarification\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"original_utterance\": request.utterance,\n            \"original_bead_body\": original_bead_body,\n            \"violations\": violation_messages,\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, validate, create bead (or approval card), return result\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n            or approval card (if approval required)\n            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n\n            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n\n            if auto_approve:\n                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n\n                # Execute directly without bead creation\n                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n\n                # Update intent in store\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    status=\"resolved\",\n                )\n\n                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n                return EscalateResult(\n                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n                    status=\"completed\",\n                )\n\n            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n\n            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 4.5: Validate and prepare for approval (NEW)\n            validation_result, reformulated_body = await self._validate_and_prepare_approval(\n                request=request,\n                bead_body=bead_body,\n                bead_type=bead_type,\n            )\n\n            # Use reformulated body if provided, otherwise original\n            final_body = reformulated_body or bead_body\n\n            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, final_body, bead_type)\n\n            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n            await self._create_bead_watch(\n                bead_ref=bead_id,\n                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            )\n\n            # Reset re-formulation count on successful bead creation\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.reset_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n            logger.info(f\"Reset reformulation_count for session {request.session_id} after successful bead creation\")\n\n            # Step 6: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except BeadApprovalRequired as e:\n            # Approval required - store pending approval and broadcast approval event\n            logger.info(f\"Approval required for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            # Update intent status\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"awaiting_approval\",\n            )\n\n            # Store pending approval in database\n            approval_id = await store.create_pending_approval(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                session_id=request.session_id,\n                bead_body=e.bead_body,\n                bead_type=e.bead_type,\n                validation_result=e.approval_card.get(\"validation_result\", {}),\n                utterance=request.utterance,\n                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n                topic_id=request.topic_id,\n            )\n            logger.info(f\"Stored pending approval {approval_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            # Add approval_id to approval card for reference\n            e.approval_card[\"approval_id\"] = approval_id\n\n            # Broadcast approval_required event to canvas\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.APPROVAL_REQUIRED,\n                    data={\n                        \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n                        \"approval_id\": approval_id,\n                        \"approval_card\": e.approval_card,\n                        \"bead_body\": e.bead_body,\n                        \"bead_type\": e.bead_type,\n                        \"utterance\": for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created yet\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=e.approval_card,\n                status=\"awaiting_approval\",\n            )\n        except ValidationRetryExhaustedError as e:\n            # Re-formulation failed - return clarification card\n            logger.warning(f\"Re-formulation exhausted for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Update intent status\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"needs_clarification\",\n            )\n\n            # Build clarification card\n            # We need to get the original bead body that was formulated before validation\n            # Since we don't have the original bead body at this point, we use the utterance\n            clarification_card = self.build_clarification_card(\n                request=request,\n                original_bead_body=request.utterance,  # Fallback to utterance\n              )\n            )\n            logger.info(f\"Broadcast clarification_card event for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=\"\",  # failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: failed card in session store\n    - Broadcasts task_failed SSE event\n\n    Args:\n        intent_id: The intent that failed\n        session_id: The session ID\n        topic_id: The topic ID  intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"Failed to fetch intent for result_type derivation: {e}\")\n\n    final_topic_id = topic_id\n    if Lowercase for pattern matching\n        body_lower = bead_body.lower()\n\n        # Check for information beads (no approval needed)\n        is_informational = self._is_informational_bead(bead_body, body_lower)\n        if is_informational and bead_type_enum == BeadType.TASK:\n            logger.info(\"Bead appears to be informational - no approval required\")\n            return ValidationResult.valid(BeadType.INFORMATIONAL)\n\n        violations = []\n\n        # Exception: self_modification and monitoring_config beads are supposed to\n        # modify prompts/configs directly, not through GitOps infrastructure workflow\n        if self._is_mutation_bead(body_lower) and bead_type_enum not in (\n            BeadType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            BeadType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n        ):\n            gitops_violations = self._check_gitops_requirement(bead_body, body_lower)\n            violations.extend(gitops_violations)\n\n        # Rule 3: Check scoping requirements\n        scoping_violations = self._check_scoping_requirement(bead_body, body_lower)\n        violations.extend(scoping_violations)\n\n        # If there are ERROR-level violations, bead is invalid\n        error_violations = [v for v in violations if v.severity == Severity.ERROR]\n        if error_violations:\n            hint = self._generate_reformulation_hint(error_violations)\n            logger.warning(f\"Bead validation failed with {len(error_violations)} errors\")\n            return ValidationResult.invalid(error_violations, reformulation_hint=hint)\n\n        # Rule 4: Action beads require approval\n        if bead_type_enum == BeadType.ACTION:\n            warning_violations = [v for v in violations if v.severity == Severity.WARNING]\n            logger.info(f\"Action bead requires approval ({len(warning_violations)} warnings)\")\n            return ValidationResult.requires_approval(\n                bead_type=bead_type_enum,\n                reason=\"Action-type beads require explicit user approval\",\n                violations=warning_violations,\n            )\n\n        # Look for informational keywords in title/summary\n        lines = bead_body.split(\"\\n\")\n        first_few_lines = \"\\n\".join(lines[:5])\n\n        # Check for informational patterns\n        for pattern in INFORMATIONAL_PATTERNS:\n            if re.search(pattern, body_lower):\n                # But make sure it's not a mutation in disguise\n                if not any(verb in body_lower for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS):\n                    return True\n\n        return False\n\n    def _is_mutation_bead(self, body_lower: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if bead describes a mutation operation.\"\"\"\n        # Check for any forbidden kubectl verb\n        for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS:\n            if verb in body_lower:\n                return True\n\n        # Check for mutation keywords\n        mutation_keywords = [\"restart\", \"deploy\", \"scale\", \"delete\", \"update\", \"patch\"]\n        for keyword in mutation_keywords:\n            if keyword in body_lower:\n                return True\n\n        return False\n\n    def _check_forbidden_kubectl_verbs(\n        self,\n        bead_body: str,\n        body_lower: str,\n    ) -> list[Violation]:\n        \"\"\"Check for forbidden direct kubectl mutation commands.\"\"\"\n        violations = []\n\n        for verb in FORBIDDEN_KUBECTL_VERBS:\n            # Look for kubectl followed by forbidden verb\n            pattern = rf\"\\bkubectl\\s+{re.escape(verb)}\\b\"\n            matches = list(re.finditer(pattern, body_lower, re.MULTILINE))\n\n            for match in matches:\n                # Extract line number\n                line_num = body_lower[:match.start()].count(\"\\n\") + 1\n                line_text = self._get_line_context(bead_body, line_num)\n\n                violations.append(Violation(\n                    rule_id=\"no_direct_kubectl_mutation\",\n                    severity=Severity.ERROR,\n                    message=f\"Direct kubectl '{verb}' command detected.               }\n                    await voice_session.push_result(result_data)\n                    await push_to_canvas(session_id, result_data, audio_surface_id)\n                    logger.info(f\"Narrated result {r.result_id} (batched)\")\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Failed to narrate result {r.result_id}: {e}\")\n\n        batcher.set_narrate_callback(narrate_batch)\n\n    while True:\n        try:\n            # Check for new results\n            unsurfed = await store.get_unsurfaced_results(session_id)\n\n            newly_pushed = []\n            for result in unsurfed:\n                result_id = result[\"id\"]\n\n                # Skip if already pushed (e.g., from previous iteration after error)\n Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_escalate_prompt() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Load the escalate prompt from the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH, \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            if content.strip():\n                return content\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt file is empty: {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n                return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt not found at {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n        return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateRequest:\n    \"\"\"Request to escalate an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    intent_type: str\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    topic_id: str | None = None\n    context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"session_id\": self.session_id,\n            \"utterance\": self.utterance,\n            \"intent_type\": self.intent_type,\n            \"project_slug\": self.project_slug,\n            \"topic_id\": self.topic_id,\n            \"context\": self.context,\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of escalating an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    bead_id: str\n    intent_id: str\n    pending_card: dict\n    status: str = \"pending\"  #
